description: Okay, I think I figured out the features that I like. So instead of not being able to scroll, on a particular question you can scroll as long as it's something that's aligned on that particular side. So you can you can scroll and you can skip a particular item as long as it's on the same side of a given argument. But if you listen to the whole thing the whole one minute or however long an item is let's say if you listen to at least one minute of a particular side, then, and sorry, it has to be not just one minute of collected arguments, but you have to listen to one entire side that totals at least one minute. and then then you're automatically assigned to the next side. So basically it locks you to a particular side until you've listened to a good argument that's at least one minute long from a particular side. and actually now that I say this let's make it 30 seconds instead of one minute so 30 seconds is the minimum for any uh particular piece of content to be

results.identified_issue: Feature reshapes the core Discover lock model (DiscoverView.swift unlockedIndex/maxAllowedIndex) and inverts the deck order (ArgumentDeckBuilder currently builds strict A/B/A/B alternation; the new model wants side-grouped order so 'same side' items sit together and are freely scrollable). The 30s rule is genuinely ambiguous AND the source audio cuts off mid-sentence ('...30 seconds is the minimum for any particular piece of content to be ___'), so I planned rather than guessed. Recommended reading: 30s is a per-side accumulated total of arguments heard to completion (skipping earns nothing), which also naturally satisfies the 'one good argument at least 30s' framing since a single 30s+ item heard out crosses the threshold alone. Alternatives considered: (a) 30s as a strict per-item minimum length, (b) 30s of raw playback time regardless of skipping. Chose accumulated-completed-listening because the reporter explicitly contrasted 'not just one minute of collected arguments' with 'one entire side that totals' — i.e. whole arguments, summed. Held code until the human confirms the cut-off number and the per-item-vs-per-side question.
results.summary: Plan for side-locked scrolling: free scroll within a side, auto-advance to the other side after 30s of listening
results.detailed_description:
This is a plan — I haven't written code yet, because this reshapes the whole Discover lock model and your voice note cuts off right on the key number, so I want a green light on the exact rule before I build it.

Here's the new model as I read it:

1) Deck groups by side instead of alternating. Today Discover interleaves A/B/A/B and lets you advance exactly one card at a time. The new feed puts a side's arguments together, so 'same side' items sit next to each other and can be scrolled through.
2) Free scroll + skip WITHIN your current side. The hard no-scroll lock goes away for same-side movement — you can skip past an argument you don't like as long as the next one is on the same side.
3) You stay locked to that side until you've listened to complete arguments on it totaling at least 30 seconds. Skipping around earns nothing; only arguments heard to the end count toward the 30s.
4) The moment that side's heard-total crosses 30s, you're auto-assigned to the next side (A to B), and the same rule applies there.

The one thing I need you to confirm — your audio ends mid-sentence: '...30 seconds is the minimum for any particular piece of content to be ___'. So does 30 seconds mean (a) a per-side total summed across whole arguments you finish [my recommendation], (b) a single argument that is itself at least 30s long unlocks the side, or (c) a minimum LENGTH each argument must be to count at all? I'd go with (a): it matches your correction ('not just one minute of collected arguments — one entire side that totals'), and it still satisfies 'a good argument at least 30s long,' since finishing one 30s+ argument crosses the total on its own.

If (a) is right, my implementation plan is: (1) add a side-grouped ordering to ArgumentDeckBuilder (all of side A, then all of side B) to replace the A/B alternation for Discover; (2) replace the linear unlockedIndex/maxAllowedIndex gate in DiscoverView with per-side accounting — free landing on any page within the current side, block crossing into the next side's first card until this side's heard-total >= 30s, then auto-advance across; (3) accumulate heard-seconds per side off the player's existing finishedItemID signal (count a clip's duration only when heard to the end). Say 'go' (and confirm the 30s meaning) and I'll build it exactly this way, or correct any point and I'll adjust.
